dict.string: Echo Scribe transcribes what you say into text at your cursor. Audio is processed locally and never leaves your machine.
Echo Scribe needs Accessibility access to listen for the global dictation hotkey and paste transcribed text at your cursor in any app.
Echo Scribe synthesizes a paste keystroke to insert transcribed text at your cursor.
Echo Scribe captures system audio during meetings so it can transcribe the other participants. Only audio is read; no screen content is recorded or stored.
Echo Scribe records your camera only when you enable it for a screen recording.
Echo Scribe matches your meetings to calendar events so summaries include attendees and topic. The data never leaves your Mac.
Echo Scribe matches your meetings to calendar events so summaries include attendees and topic. The data never leaves your Mac.
